normalized_title: State Planning Policy 5.4 Road and Rail Noise
scope_summary: Minimises the adverse impact of road and rail transport noise on noise-sensitive land use and development in Western Australia, while protecting the future operation of transport corridors. Applies to planning instruments, structure plans, subdivision and development proposals where noise-sensitive land use is proposed within specified trigger distances of strategic/other significant freight and traffic routes and passenger/freight railways, and to new or major upgrades of those corridors. Sets outdoor and indoor noise targets and requires noise exposure forecasts, noise level contour maps and noise management plans.

key_numeric_standards.topic: trigger distance - strategic freight and major traffic routes
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: roads per Perth and Peel Planning Frameworks and/or >=500 Class 7-12 Austroads vehicles/day and/or 50,000 vehicles/day

key_numeric_standards.topic: trigger distance - other significant freight/traffic routes
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: State administered/future State roads and roads with >=100 Class 7-12 Austroads vehicles/day or >=23,000 daily traffic count

key_numeric_standards.unit: m from centreline of closest track
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: noise-sensitive land use near passenger railways

key_numeric_standards.unit: m from centreline of closest track
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: noise-sensitive land use near freight railways

key_numeric_standards.topic: outdoor noise target - new noise-sensitive land use/development (day)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: new noise-sensitive land use/development within trigger distance of existing/proposed transport corridor

key_numeric_standards.topic: outdoor noise target - new noise-sensitive land use/development (night)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: new noise-sensitive land use/development within trigger distance

key_numeric_standards.topic: indoor noise target - living and work areas

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: new road or railway infrastructure proposals
major upgrades of roads or railways (5 dB allowance above new-infrastructure targets)

key_numeric_standards.unit: m from most exposed habitable facade
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: measurement of noise targets for proposed buildings

definitions: Class 7 to 12 Austroads vehicles per day

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 3.1 Residential Design Codes
AS/NZS 2107:2016 Acoustics - Recommended design sound levels and reverberation times for building interiors

quality_flags: Exact gazettal date not stated in extracted text; only the publication month (September 2019) appears.
Cites the Residential Design Codes under their pre-2019 designation 'SPP 3.1' (now SPP 7.3) - mapped to PC-001.
Multi-column PDF extraction interleaves columns in places (Table 2 layout is jumbled but values recoverable).
